mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which correlates positively with near-term corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% represents a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the equity discount rate and depresses risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in unemployment above 0.2 percentage points over two months alongside a federal funds rate above 4.0% create a stagflationary drag on corporate earnings and consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over 2 months while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following joint trigger are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll expansion when unemployment is already below 4.0% signals an overheating labor market that intensifies wage-cost pressures and compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS increases are not negative when UNRATE < 4.0% (mean return >= 0 over 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price index growth exceeding 0.3% month-over-month signals persistent inflation that forces the central bank to maintain elevated real interest rates, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative following CPIAUCSL accelerations (Rank IC > -0.03 or mean return >= 0 over 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent levels of retail sales above 700000 million dollars and housing starts above 1500 thousand units indicate broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of joint-high-demand instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing activity and inventory destocking, which precedes negative earnings surprises and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are positive in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth in chained 2017 dollars reflects aggregate demand expansion that underpins corporate top-line growth and profit margins over a quarterly horizon, driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-median GDPC1 growth prints are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
